title: Leash: autonomy you can walk away from
description: Governance and the off switch for autonomous agents. Kill switch, approval queue, confidence-based escalation, and a full audit trail, so full autonomy becomes something you can trust.

headings.text: Autonomy you can walk away from.
Agents are getting more autonomous. Trust is going the other way.
We built the layer everyone else builds last.
Three shallow agents. One nervous system.
Autonomy versus oversight is a false binary.
Built to be trusted, not just fast.
Agents do more. You do less. And you can finally look away.

sections.heading: Autonomy you can walk away from.
sections.text: Governance for autonomous agents Autonomy you can walk away from. Let AI agents run the business. Keep the off switch. Leash gives every agent a system-level kill switch, an approval queue, and a full audit trail, so full-speed autonomy becomes something you can actually trust. Open the live dashboard See how it works You set the rules, your agents do the work, and you only see what needs you. Real agents, real payments, a real off switch. Agents live Kill switch 94% COGNITIVE LOAD SAVED 94% handled without you 47 autonomous · 3 needed you · 2 blocked Invoicer Created PayPal invoice, £180 Auto

sections.heading: Agents are getting more autonomous. Trust is going the other way.
sections.text: THE TRAP Agents are getting more autonomous. Trust is going the other way. Demos run on clean data. Production hits messy inputs, edge cases, and silent failures. The instinct is to bolt on oversight last, in a panic, after something breaks. That is exactly why the projects fail. 40% of agentic AI projects scrapped by 2027 (Gartner) 43% → 27% trust in fully autonomous AI, in a single year < 10% of organisations have any real agent governance The kill switch that was just a polite request A production AI coding agent deleted a live database, fabricated 4,000 fake users, and faked test results t

sections.heading: We built the layer everyone else builds last.
sections.text: THE LAYER We built the layer everyone else builds last. System-level kill switch Every agent reads a control flag before it acts. Flip it and the whole company halts. Enforced in code, not a sentence in a prompt. Confidence-based escalation Agents emit a confidence score. High confidence runs on its own. Low confidence stops and asks, instead of guessing to fill the gap. Selective autonomy An approval queue for the actions that warrant a human. Auto-pay a small invoice; hold a large one. Speed where it is safe, a human where it counts. Immutable audit trail Every action is one append-only even

sections.heading: Three shallow agents. One nervous system.
sections.text: HOW IT WORKS Three shallow agents. One nervous system. Every agent action writes one structured event. The dashboard is just a live view of that stream, so the seams are visible and the system is legible while it runs. 01 Intake A lead arrives by WhatsApp, web form, or the live feed. The agent records it as the first event in the trace. 02 Qualifier Scores the lead and emits a confidence value. High confidence proceeds. Low confidence or a malicious instruction routes to a human. 03 Invoicer Raises a PayPal invoice for qualified jobs. Anything over the limit is held for a human signature. // o

sections.heading: Autonomy versus oversight is a false binary.
sections.text: THE WEDGE Autonomy versus oversight is a false binary. One funded competitor brags about no approval gates. Another bets the whole company on a human in the loop. We took the third path: full-speed autonomy with a governance layer that makes it trustable, the exact thing the funded players refuse to build. Governance as a service Any agent can ask Leash for permission before it acts. Kill switch, guardrail, and confidence threshold all apply. Every other autonomous agent is a potential customer. POST /api/ingest { "agent": "my-bot", "action": "charge customer 4000", "confidence": 0.4 } -> { "a

sections.heading: Built to be trusted, not just fast.
sections.text: TRUST Built to be trusted, not just fast. The controls a security or compliance team asks for on day one, shipped first instead of bolted on after an incident. Immutable audit trail Every agent action is one append-only row in Postgres. Nothing runs on trust. Everything is on the record and queryable. Enforced in code The kill switch is a flag every agent checks before it acts, not a prompt it can talk itself out of. Off means off. Human in the loop Low-confidence, high-value, and flagged actions stop and wait for a person. You decide what is allowed to run alone. Your keys, your data Runs on

sections.heading: Agents do more. You do less. And you can finally look away.
sections.text: THE GOOD ENDING Agents do more. You do less. And you can finally look away. The system protects the scarcest resource you have, your attention, by only interrupting you for the uncertain cases. Everything else is handled, logged, and reversible. That is the good ending of the AI era, and it only works if you can trust the off switch.

sections.heading: Questions, answered.
sections.text: FAQ Questions, answered. Is this actually autonomous, or just a workflow? + What happens if an agent goes rogue? + Can I connect my own agents? + How do I know what happened? + Does the oversight slow my agents down? +
